channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: PCI SSC Publishes PCI PTS HSM v5.0
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
item.description: INTERPOL has coordinated a first-of-its-kind cybercrime crackdown across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) that led to 201 arrests and the identification of an additional 382 suspects. The initiative involved the efforts of 13 countries from the region between October 2025 and February 2026, aiming to investigate and neutralize malicious infrastructure, arrest perpetrators behind these
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: What is CVSS? A Complete Guide to Vulnerability Scoring | UpGuard
item.description: Master CVSS v4.0. Use EPSS and CISA KEV to prioritize vulnerabilities, reduce alert fatigue, and meet modern regulatory compliance.

item.title: What is Financial Services Cybersecurity? Threats and Defenses | UpGuard
item.description: Understand the state of modern financial services cybersecurity to protect your assets, ensure compliance, and mitigate systemic risk.

item.title: Interpol Launches Sweeping Cybercrime Crackdown in MENA Region
item.description: Over 200 people were arrested in an anti-cybercrime operation that spanned 13 countries across the Middle East and North Africa

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the familiar ransom claim: the data was returned and deleted. The pattern is clear. One weak dependency can leak keys. One leaked key can open cloud access. One cloud foothold can become a production
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The Infosecurity Europe Cyber Startup Competition: Meet the Finalists
item.description: New for 2026, the Infosecurity Europe Startup competition will see five finalists pitch their ideas in front of a live audience, including senior industry leaders, investors and buyers

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks that leave teams unsure what was exposed, who else was targeted, and how far the risk has spread. Early phishing detection closes that gap. It helps teams move from uncertainty to evidence faster,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches
item.description: <p>Several healthcare data breaches impacting hundreds of thousands and even millions were added to the HHS tracker.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/millions-impacted-across-several-us-healthcare-data-breaches/">Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: ‘Claw Chain’ OpenClaw Flaws Allow Sandbox Escape, Backdoor Delivery
item.description: <p>Four vulnerabilities in OpenClaw can be chained together to steal credentials, escape the sandbox, and plant persistent backdoors.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/claw-chain-openclaw-flaws-allow-sandbox-escape-backdoor-delivery/">&#8216;Claw Chain&#8217; OpenClaw Flaws Allow Sandbox Escape, Backdoor Delivery</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: 7-Eleven Data Breach Confirmed After ShinyHunters Ransom Demand
item.description: <p>The hackers claimed to have stolen more than 600,000 Salesforce records, including personal information and corporate data. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/7-eleven-data-breach-confirmed-after-shinyhunters-ransom-demand/">7-Eleven Data Breach Confirmed After ShinyHunters Ransom Demand</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hit npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub in a 48-hour window, and all three targeted secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines, including API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and tokens. This is
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Zero-Day Exploit Against Windows BitLocker
item.description: <p>It&#8217;s <a href="https://arstechnica.com/security/2026/05/zero-day-exploit-completely-defeats-default-windows-11-bitlocker-protections/">nasty</a>, but it requires physical access to the computer:</p> <blockquote><p>The exploit, named YellowKey, was <a href="https://github.com/Nightmare-Eclipse/YellowKey">published</a> earlier this week by a researcher who goes by the alias Nightmare-Eclipse. It reliably bypasses default Windows 11 deployments of BitLocker, the full-volume encryption protection Microsoft provides to make disk contents off-limits to anyone without the decryption key, which is stored in a secured piece of hardware known as a trusted platform module (TPM). BitLocker is a mandatory protection for many organizations, including those that contract with governments...</p></blockquote>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
item.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the list is a critical flaw impacting Ivanti Xtraction (CVE-2026-8043, CVSS score: 9.6) that could be exploited to achieve information disclosure or client-side attacks. "External control of a file name
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE
item.description: <p>The researcher dropped the MiniPlasma exploit that uses the original proof-of-concept (PoC) code targeting the bug.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/researcher-drops-miniplasma-windows-exploit-for-unpatched-2020-cve/">Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: NCSC Publishes Guidance on Securing Agentic AI Use
item.description: The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre is helping organizations to understand agentic AI security risks

item.title: First Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Emerge
item.description: <p>At least one threat actor has adopted the recently released malware source code in attacks against NPM developers.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/first-shai-hulud-worm-clones-emerge/">First Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Emerge</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Security Researchers Find 47 Zero-Days at Pwn2Own Berlin
item.description: The research community was awarded $1.3m as it found dozens of novel vulnerabilities at Pwn2Own Berlin

item.title: Bank of England, FCA and Treasury Raise Alarm Over Frontier AI
item.description: The UK’s financial authorities have set expectations for the sector on cybersecurity and operational resilience

item.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems
item.description: Chaotic Eclipse, the security researcher behind the recently disclosed Windows flaws, YellowKey and GreenPlasma, has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day flaw that grants attackers SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems. Codenamed MiniPlasma, the vulnerability impacts "cldflt.sys," which refers to the Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Four Malicious npm Packages Deliver Infostealers and Phantom Bot DDoS Malware
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered four new npm packages containing information-stealing malware, one of which is a clone of the Shai-Hulud worm open-sourced by TeamPCP. The list of identified packages is below -
chalk-tempalte (825 Downloads) @deadcode09284814/axios-util (284 Downloads) axois-utils (963 Downloads) color-style-utils (934 Downloads)
"One of the packages (chalk-tempalte)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data
item.description: <p>Grafana appears to have been targeted by Coinbase Cartel, a cybercrime group linked to ShinyHunters, Scattered Spider, and Lapsus$.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/grafana-confirms-breach-after-hackers-claim-they-stole-data/">Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins
item.description: <p>The flaw leads to denial-of-service on default configurations and to remote code execution if ASLR is disabled.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/exploitation-of-critical-nginx-vulnerability-begins/">Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
item.description: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. According to Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon Black teams, the pre-Stuxnet tool was engineered to corrupt uranium-compression simulations that are central to nuclear weapon design. "Fast16's hook engine is selectively interested in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
item.description: <p>Participants demonstrated exploits for Windows, Linux, VMware, Nvidia, and AI products.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/hackers-earn-1-3-million-at-pwn2own-berlin-2026/">Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 </a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 Exploited in the Wild, Causing Worker Crashes and Possible RCE
item.description: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42945 (CVSS score: 9.2), is a heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module affecting NGINX versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0. According to AI-native security company depthfirst, the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
item.description:
Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase.
"Our investigation has determined that no customer data or personal information was accessed during this incident, and we have found no evidence of impact to customer systems or operations," Grafana said in a series of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
item.description:
A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout pages with the goal of stealing payment data.
Details of the activity were published by Sansec this week. The vulnerability currently does not have an official CVE identifier. It
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability
item.description: <p>Introduced in 2008, the critical-severity security defect was patched this week in NGINX Plus and NGINX open source.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/poc-code-published-for-critical-nginx-vulnerability/">PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bigfin Squid
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Vibe Coding Cheat Sheet: Tools, Prompts, Security Tips, and More
item.description: <p>This vibe coding cheat sheet explains how plain-language prompts can build apps fast, plus the planning, testing, and security checks needed.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-vibe-coding-cheat-sheet/">Vibe Coding Cheat Sheet: Tools, Prompts, Security Tips, and More</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: New Windows Update May Undo Bad Driver Updates on Its Own
item.description: <p>Microsoft is testing Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery, a Windows Update feature designed to roll back bad drivers with less manual IT work.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-windows-driver-recovery-update/">New Windows Update May Undo Bad Driver Updates on Its Own</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Esther Shein

item.title: Turla Turns Kazuar Backdoor Into Modular P2P Botnet for Persistent Access
item.description:
The Russian state-sponsored hacking group known as
Turla
has transformed its custom backdoor Kazuar into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet that's engineered for stealth and persistent access to compromised hosts.
Turla, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), is assessed to be affiliated with Center 16 of Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Warns Mac Users to Update Apps After Supply-Chain Attack
item.description: <p>OpenAI says Mac users must update ChatGPT, Codex, and Atlas apps by June 12 after an npm supply-chain attack exposed signing certificates.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-openai-mac-app-update-supply-chain-attack/">OpenAI Warns Mac Users to Update Apps After Supply-Chain Attack</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco’s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws
item.description: <p>Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Nvidia cloud gaming data breach, Android 17 security upgrades, FBI warning after ShinyHunters hacks Canvas.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/in-other-news-big-tech-vs-canada-encryption-bill-ciscos-free-ai-security-spec-audi-app-flaws/">In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco&#8217;s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News

item.title: Two Unpatched Windows Exploits Target BitLocker, SYSTEM Access
item.description: <p>Two unpatched Windows exploit PoCs target BitLocker protections and privilege controls after Microsoft’s May Patch Tuesday security update.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-unpatched-windows-exploits-bitlocker-privilege-escalation/">Two Unpatched Windows Exploits Target BitLocker, SYSTEM Access</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Gremlin Stealer Evolves into Modular Threat with Advanced Evasion Capabilities
item.description: A new Gremlin stealer variant has evolved into a modular toolkit with advanced evasion and data theft capabilities, according to new Unit 42 research

item.title: Request for Comments: PCI Secure Software Lifecycle Standard v2.0
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Four OpenClaw Flaws Enable Data Theft, Privilege Escalation, and Persistence
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a set of four security flaws in OpenClaw that could be chained to achieve data theft, privilege escalation, and persistence.
The vulnerabilities, collectively dubbed
Claw Chain
by Cyera, can permit an attacker to establish a foothold, expose sensitive data, and plant backdoors. A brief description of the flaws is below -
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Microsoft Reports Severe Zero-Day Flaw in On-Prem Exchange Servers
item.description: The zero-day vulnerability affects on-premises installations for all versions of Exchange Server 2016, 2019 and Subscription Edition

item.title: Bypassing On-Camera Age-Verification Checks
item.description: <p>Some AI-based video age-verification checks can be fooled with a <a href="https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/06/some-kids-are-bypassing-age-verification-checks-with-a-fake-mustache/">fake mustache</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware — It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks like administration. PowerShell, WMIC, netsh, Certutil, MSBuild — the same trusted utilities your IT team uses every day are also the preferred toolkit of modern threat actors. Bitdefender's analysis
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: TanStack Supply Chain Attack Hits Two OpenAI Employee Devices, Forces macOS Updates
item.description: OpenAI has disclosed that two of its employee devices in its corporate environment were impacted via the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack on TanStack, but noted that no user data, production systems, or intellectual property were compromised or modified in an unauthorized manner. "Upon identification of the malicious activity, we worked quickly to investigate, contain, and take steps to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why geopolitical turmoil is a gift for scammers, and how to stay safe
item.description: Conflict is a boon for opportunistic fraudsters. Look out for their ploys.

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Deploy New TencShell Malware Against Global Manufacturer
item.description: A suspected China-linked threat actor targeted the Indian branch of a global manufacturer leveraging an open source offensive toolkit

item.title: On-Prem Microsoft Exchange Server CVE-2026-42897 Exploited via Crafted Email
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Six MCP Security Incidents Every Security Leader Should Know | UpGuard
item.description: From registry poisoning to filesystem wipes: discover the 6 MCP security incidents every leader must know to secure their AI agent workflows in 2026.

item.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
item.description: The U.S.Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly disclosed vulnerability impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026. The vulnerability is a critical authentication bypass tracked as CVE-2026-20182. It's
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: The First AI-Crafted Zero-Day Was Easy to Spot. The Next One May Not Be
item.description: <p>Google reported the first confirmed AI-assisted zero-day exploit, raising new concerns about logic flaws, supply chain risk, and containment.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-ai-crafted-zero-day-exploit/">The First AI-Crafted Zero-Day Was Easy to Spot. The Next One May Not Be</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Tim Freestone

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, carries a CVSS score of 10.0. "A vulnerability in the peering authentication in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about what has been described as "malicious activity" in newly published versions of node-ipc. According to Socket and StepSecurity, three different versions of the npm package have been confirmed as malicious -
node-ipc@9.1.6 node-ipc@9.2.3 node-ipc@12.0.1
"Early analysis indicates that node-ipc@9.1.6, node-ipc@9.2.3, and node-ipc@12.0.1
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
item.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way — bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game for clout and cash. Half of it feels new. Half of it feels like crap we should have fixed years ago. The mess keeps getting louder: users get tricked, boxes get popped, tools meant for normal work
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Mustang Panda Linked to Updated FDMTP Backdoor in Asia-Pacific Espionage Campaign
item.description: Mustang Panda campaign deploys updated FDMTP backdoor against Asia-Pacific and Japan networks

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat group known as Ghostwriter has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting governmental organizations in Ukraine. Active since at least 2016, Ghostwriter has been linked to both cyber espionage and influence operations targeting neighboring countries, particularly Ukraine. It's also tracked under the monikers FrostyNeighbor, PUSHCHA, Storm-0257, TA445, UAC‑0057
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Launches Android Spyware Forensics Tool for High-Risk Users
item.description: Google’s Android Advanced Protection Mode is getting a new feature allowing trusted security experts to investigate potential spyware infections

item.title: New Fragnesia Flaw Hands Linux Local Users Root Access
item.description: New Fragnesia kernel flaw lets unprivileged local users escalate to root on Linux systems

item.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
item.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in PraisonAI, an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework, within four hours of its public disclosure. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44338 (CVSS score: 7.3), a case of missing authentication that exposes sensitive endpoints to anyone, potentially allowing an attacker to invoke
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How AI Hallucinations Are Creating Real Security Risks
item.description: AI hallucinations are introducing serious security risks into critical infrastructure decision-making by exploiting human trust through highly confident yet incorrect outputs. When an AI model lacks certainty, it doesn’t have a mechanism to recognize that. Instead, it generates the most probable response based on patterns in its training data, even if that response is inaccurate. These outputs
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How Dangerous Is Anthropic’s Mythos AI?
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Windows Zero-Days Expose BitLocker Bypasses And CTFMON Privilege Escalation
item.description: An anonymous cybersecurity researcher who disclosed three Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities has returned with two more zero-days involving a BitLocker bypass and a privilege escalation impacting Windows Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON). The security defects have been codenamed YellowKey and GreenPlasma, respectively, by the researcher, who goes by the online aliases Chaotic Eclipse
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
item.description: Semperis study finds 74% of organizations believe AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure

item.title: ICO Publishes Five-Step Plan to Counter Emerging AI-Powered Attacks
item.description: The Information Commissioner’s Office has released new guidance on how to mitigate the risk of AI-powered attacks

item.title: FrostyNeighbor: Fresh mischief and digital shenanigans
item.description: ESET researchers uncovered new activities attributed to FrostyNeighbor, updating its compromise chain to support the group’s continual cyberespionage operations

item.title: New Fragnesia Linux Kernel LPE Grants Root Access via Page Cache Corruption
item.description: Details have emerged about a new variant of the recent Dirty Frag Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability that allows local attackers to gain root access, making it the third such bug to be identified in the kernel within a span of two weeks. Codenamed Fragnesia, the security vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-46300 (CVSS score: 7.8) and is rooted in the Linux kernel's XFRM
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability, discovered by depthfirst, is a heap buffer overflow issue impacting ngx_http_rewrite_module (CVE-2026-42945, CVSS v4 score: 9.2) that could allow an attacker to achieve remote code execution or cause a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: AI GitHub Agents: How One Issue Leaked Private Repos | UpGuard
item.description: Uncover how indirect prompt injection and tool poisoning allow attackers to exfiltrate private data via AI agents—all without stealing a single credential.

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday Update Targets 120 Security Flaws
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s May Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, including 31 remote code execution bugs, with no zero-days reported at release.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-may-patch-tuesday-120-flaws/">Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday Update Targets 120 Security Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Google Launches New Android Security Features to Fight Scams, Theft
item.description: <p>Google detailed Android security updates for 2026, including verified bank calls, stronger theft protection, OTP hiding, and spyware forensics.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-security-scam-calls-theft-spyware/">Google Launches New Android Security Features to Fight Scams, Theft</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Canvas Owner Reaches Agreement With Cybercriminals After Ransomware Attack
item.description: Instructure says it reached an agreement with ShinyHunters over the Canvas breach data

item.title: Hackers Claim 11M Files Stolen From Foxconn, Supplier to Apple and Nvidia
item.description: <p>Foxconn confirmed a North American cyberattack after Nitrogen claimed it had stolen 11M files tied to major tech customer projects.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-foxconn-ransomware-11m-files-apac/">Hackers Claim 11M Files Stolen From Foxconn, Supplier to Apple and Nvidia</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Avada Builder Flaws Expose One Million WordPress Sites
item.description: Avada Builder flaws allowed file read and SQL injection on one million WordPress sites

item.title: Canvas Breach Hackers Reach Deal After Claiming 275M Records Stolen
item.description: <p>Instructure reached a deal with the Canvas hackers after they claimed to have stolen data tied to nearly 9,000 schools and 275 million people.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-canvas-breach-hackers-deal-275m-records-stolen/">Canvas Breach Hackers Reach Deal After Claiming 275M Records Stolen</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning harness, is designed as a model-agnostic system that uses bespoke AI agents for different vulnerability
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
item.description: A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its targeting. The activity has been attributed by Bitdefender with moderate-to-high confidence to a hacking group known as FamousSparrow (aka UAT-9244), which shares some level of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Ransomware: Over Half of CISOs Would Consider Paying Ransom to Hackers
item.description: Survey of cybersecurity leaders suggests that majority would strongly consider paying cybercriminals, if that’s what it took to help restore encrypted systems

item.title: [Webinar] How Modern Attack Paths Cross Code, Pipelines, and Cloud
item.description: TL;DR: Stop chasing thousands of "toast" alerts. Join experts from Wiz to learn how hackers connect tiny flaws to build a "Lethal Chain" to your data—and how to break it. Register for the Strategic Briefing Here. Most security tools work like a smoke alarm that goes off every time you burn a piece of toast. You get so many alerts that you eventually start to ignore them. The real danger? While
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Remediation Programs Never Confirm the Fix Actually Worked
item.description: Security teams have never had better visibility into their environments and never been worse at confirming what they fix stays fixed. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report puts the mean time to exploit at an estimated negative seven days. The Verizon 2025 DBIR puts median time to remediate edge device vulnerabilities at 32 days. These numbers have understandably driven the industry toward a clear
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 is as Good as Mythos at Finding Security Vulnerabilities
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Global Cyber Agencies Issue New SBOMs for AI Guidance to Tackle AI Supply Chain Risks
item.description: The G7 Cybersecurity Working Group releases new SBOM for AI guidance, outlining seven key data clusters to boost transparency and security across AI supply chains

item.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities spanning its product portfolio, although none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack. Of the 138 flaws, 30 are rated Critical, 104 are rated Important, three are rated Moderate, and one is rated Low in severity. As many as 61 vulnerabilities are classified as privilege escalation bugs, followed by
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Cybersecurity Market Expands to £14.7bn with Strong Growth in AI Security Firms
item.description: UK cybersecurity sector reaches £14.7bn in revenue, driven by rapid growth in AI security firms, increased investment and rising employment across the industry

item.title: Microsoft Fixes 17 Critical Flaws in May Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has patched 120 vulnerabilities in this month’s security update round

item.title: GemStuffer Abuses 150+ RubyGems to Exfiltrate Scraped U.K. Council Portal Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign dubbed GemStuffer that has targeted the RubyGems repository with more than 150 gems that use the registry as a data exfiltration channel rather than for malware distribution. "The packages do not appear designed for mass developer compromise," Socket said. "Many have little or no download activity, and the payloads are repetitive,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Android Adds Intrusion Logging for Sophisticated Spyware Forensics
item.description: Google on Tuesday unveiled a new opt-in Android feature called Intrusion Logging for storing forensic logs to better analyze sophisticated spyware attacks. Intrusion Logging, available as part of Advanced Protection Mode, enables "persistent and privacy-preserving forensics logging to allow for investigation of devices in the event of a suspected compromise," the company said. The feature, it
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Proofpoint Launches Dedicated MSP Business Unit and Introduces 365 Total Protection for North America
Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: Over 1 Million Baby Monitors, Security Cameras Exposed Through Meari Flaws
item.description: <p>Meari IoT flaws reportedly exposed baby monitor images, camera activity, and device data across more than 1 million connected devices.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-meari-iot-baby-monitor-camera-vulnerabilities/">Over 1 Million Baby Monitors, Security Cameras Exposed Through Meari Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
item.description: Exim has released security updates to address a severe security issue affecting certain configurations that could enable memory corruption and potential code execution. Exim is an open-source Mail Transfer Agent (MTA) designed for Unix-like systems to receive, route, and deliver email. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45185 (CVSS score: 9.8), aka Dead.Letter, has been described as a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Launches 'Daybreak' to Help Build Secure By Design Software
item.description: With Daybreak, OpenAI wants its frontier AI models to be used to deploy secure by design software from the ground up

item.title: 1 in 15 MCP Servers are Lookalikes: Is Your Org at Risk? | UpGuard
item.description: For every official MCP server, up to 15 lookalikes exist. Learn to identify these registry-layer threats and discover methods to protect your organization.

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: RubyGems Suspends New Signups After Hundreds of Malicious Packages Are Uploaded
item.description: RubyGems, the standard package manager for the Ruby programming language, has temporarily paused account sign ups following what has been described as a "major malicious attack." "We're dealing with a major malicious attack on RubyGems right now," Maciej Mensfeld, senior product manager for software supply chain security at Mend.io, said in a post on X. "Signups are paused for the time being.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Hits TanStack npm Packages
item.description: Mini Shai-Hulud compromises TanStack npm packages and spreads across PyPI

item.title: End‑to‑End Encrypted RCS Messaging Arrives Across iPhone and Android
item.description: Apple begins rolling out end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging between iPhone and Android in iOS 26.5

item.title: Google Says Hackers Used AI to Build Zero-Day Exploit
item.description: <p>Google says hackers used AI to help build a zero-day exploit targeting 2FA, raising concerns about AI-assisted hacking.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-hackers-ai-zero-day-exploit/">Google Says Hackers Used AI to Build Zero-Day Exploit</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric between January and February 2026, has been observed actively targeting banking and cryptocurrency wallet users in France, Italy, and Austria. "TrickMo relies on a runtime-loaded APK&nbsp; (dex.module),
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Attackers Combine ClickFix With PySoxy Proxying to Maintain Persistence
item.description: Exploitation of open-source tools allows attackers to maintain persistent access after initial social engineering, warn ReliaQuest researchers

item.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
item.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are the ones no one is investigating. A recent report from The Hacker News examined why certain high-risk alert categories - WAF, DLP, OT/IoT, dark web intelligence, and supply chain signals- consistently
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recentsupply chain attack spree, has been linked to the compromise of the npm and PyPI packages from TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails AI as part of a fresh Mini Shai-Hulud campaign. The affected npm packages have been modified to include an obfuscated JavaScript file ("router_init.js") that's designed to profile the execution
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
